output:
No. Method 2 is more correct.

In this hypothetical world, respiratory issues and broken bones are independent causes of hospitalization status — there is no direct causal link between respiratory issues and broken bones. Hospitalization status is a **collider** (a common effect of both).

Method 1 conditions on hospitalization status, which opens a spurious association between respiratory issues and broken bones (collider bias / "explaining away"). Method 2 looks at the direct relationship without conditioning on the collider, which correctly reflects that there is no causal effect of respiratory issues on broken bones.
